Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: joe-broadhead
Message:
World-class gap closure: flat hot-path latency, observability, security depth, supply-chain & type hardening (#174)

* World-class gaps wave 1: persistence, DX, CI tooling, observability

Persistence: long-lived per-path SQLite connection with a prepared-
statement cache (removes per-op open/PRAGMA/chmod/recompile overhead),
idempotent task creation via source_key, CHECK constraints on status/
priority/dependency enums, guarded JSON parsing on the durable ledger
path, a typed SqliteRow helper on the hot read path, and O(1) run
detail via a targeted query; disposeWorkStore() wired into shutdown.

DX: a gateway_catalog discovery tool covering all 148 MCP tools, tiered
CLI help with --version / exit-2 unknown-command / per-subcommand help,
and a generated MCP + OpenAPI reference from the tool/route registry.

CI/supply-chain: Dependabot, npm audit, CycloneDX SBOM, Trivy image
scan, cosign keyless signing + provenance, a Biome lint/format gate
(0 violations), coverage tooling, a weekly Stryker mutation workflow,
and a performance-budget CI step.

Observability: a structured leveled logger (timestamps, correlation
ids, secret redaction), a Prometheus /metrics endpoint with retained
SLO histograms, process runtime self-metrics, and proactive
leadership-lease-stuck and disk-space alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* World-class gaps wave 2: flat hot-path latency + test engineering

Persistence: window the mutation hot path so mutateWorkState
materializes only live runs (running, current-run-referenced, and a
bounded recent terminal slice via a new started_at index) instead of
every run ever recorded — mutation latency is now flat ~2.4ms
regardless of history (was ~82ms at 30k runs), with all run history
still durable and queryable via targeted SQL (run-by-id, per-task,
per-roadmap, SQL cost/token aggregates). Delete and env-cleanup paths
handle out-of-window runs explicitly. Idempotency key/source threaded
through the MCP create tools end to end.

Testing: a coverage ratchet (v8, floors just under the current
86.5%), property-based tests (state machine, idempotency, serialization
round-trips, config normalization), fuzzing of the Telegram/webhook/
config parsers, golden data-bearing forward-migration fixtures, and a
typed OpenCode SDK fake so provider drift breaks compilation. Retired
the --maxWorkers=1 pin for isolated forks (~3x faster suite).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* World-class gaps wave 3: security depth + read-path aggregation

Security (all additive, default-off so the single-operator localhost
flow is unchanged): exposed-mode request rate limiting, consecutive-
auth-failure lockout, and a startup token-entropy floor; dry-run
blast-radius previews for destructive tools (task/roadmap delete, bulk
update, restore, config update) that mutate nothing; an option to
reject destructive human-gate approvals arriving over the MCP trust
tier so an agent cannot self-approve; and an opt-in capability-scoped
loopback that requires a token instead of auto-trusting local requests.

Read path: governance budget evaluation (called every scheduler cycle)
no longer materializes and JS-reduces every run — it prices each budget
window with batched SQL aggregates, dropping from O(cumulative runs)
(~76ms at 30k) to a bounded ~5-7ms. roadmap-memory, product-onboarding,
observability, and storage read paths repointed to targeted/bounded
run queries; a windowed loadWorkState('live') loader is ready for the
scheduler tick. Further typed-row and JSON-guard cleanup in the store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* World-class gaps wave 4: bounded scheduler reads + architecture/craft

Perf: window the scheduler's per-tick reads to live runs (active,
current-run-referenced, recent-terminal slice), completing the hot-path
windowing — a full cycle's read cost is now ~18x lower at 30k terminal
runs and flat with history; reads that legitimately reach old runs
(retained-environment capacity, dependency patch harvesting) stay
full-scope by design. Idle scheduler wakeups coarsen from a fixed 1s
poll to a self-rescheduling timer (10-300x fewer idle wakeups). The
scheduler test fake is typed against the SDK so drift breaks the build.

Architecture: fence the design-only worker-fleet surface (~2.8k lines)
into a labeled src/preview/ boundary and mark the CLI/docs as preview;
remove the self-referential domain-boundary register and dead
descriptor gates (~320 lines) while keeping the real boundary checks.

Craft: a config zod validation layer over the normalizer (proven
parity), a safe-by-default HTML tagged template with the highest-risk
dashboard sites migrated byte-identically, a canonical GatewayError
hierarchy with a transient/permanent/fatal categorizer, and a typed
env accessor. Suite runs at maxWorkers=2 for CI subprocess headroom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* World-class gaps wave 5: stricter TypeScript + changelog

Enable noFallthroughCasesInSwitch, noImplicitOverride,
noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature, and noUncheckedIndexedAccess,
fixing ~2,177 resulting type issues with real guards (no `as any`);
exactOptionalPropertyTypes is left off because closing it cleanly would
churn persisted work-store shapes. Record the gap pass in the
changelog. Suite pinned to maxWorkers=2 for CI subprocess headroom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review findings on the world-class gap PR

An 8-angle adversarial review confirmed the core windowing, mechanical
changes, fence/deletion, and wiring are sound, and surfaced edge bugs
(mostly in the new opt-in/observability code) now fixed:

- Persistence/windowing: derive "task has ever run" from the durable
  store (not the windowed set) so a re-dispatched aged-out task is not
  spuriously start-gated; re-verify the env-cleanup fallback run by
  exact id (escaped LIKE prefilter) so a nested-substring collision
  can't mutate the wrong run; pin in-use connections so LRU eviction
  cannot close a handle mid-transaction; clear the init-state set on
  restore so the forward-only schema guard still fires; classify aged-
  out terminal runs via a by-id fallback.
- Observability: deep-redact and crash-proof the JSON logger; render
  /metrics from the last sample without resampling/resetting the lag
  window that feeds alerts; majority-based event-loop-lag alert;
  reject non-decimal env ints; cap error cause-chain serialization.
- Security honesty/UX: the CLI now presents its admin token so the
  opt-in capability-scoped loopback doesn't lock the operator out;
  documented the honest limits of the opt-in controls and the token-
  entropy startup migration.
- CI/supply-chain: strip the base image's npm bundle so the Trivy scan
  is clean (runtime never invokes npm); make coverage inherit the 20s
  timeout / 2 workers; add a generated-API-docs drift gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
